title: Publish an Agent
subtitle: Choose whether to submit a Docker image first, then fill in the public listing and the matching integration material. Image submissions go through the trusted audit path; hosted API/MCP submissions start as a low-friction listing path.

mode.title: Will you submit a Docker image?
mode.description: This choice determines how much verifiable trust evidence AgentLens can attach to your Agent. Closed-source teams can start without an image; higher-risk Agents and teams seeking stronger trust tiers should submit a private image for audit.
mode.trustDescription: The public README is user-facing. Audit material is for the platform sandbox. Keeping them separate avoids exposing private implementation details as marketing copy.

native-image.title: Submit a Docker image for trusted audit
native-image.subtitle: Best for high-trust, higher-risk Agents and recommendation placement
native-image.body: AgentLens pulls the image from the manifest, runs it in the sandbox, audits network boundaries, behavior evidence and resource usage, then binds manifest hash + image digest into an Agent identity fingerprint.

hosted-api.title: Skip the image and list a hosted API/MCP Agent
hosted-api.subtitle: Best for closed-source SaaS, early validation and externally hosted Agents
hosted-api.body: You run the service. AgentLens routes through a gateway for access control, metering, health checks and black-box tests. The platform does not receive an image, so the trust tier stays below the audited image path.

readme.title: README is user-facing
readme.body: Capabilities, scenarios, limits, examples and integration details feed the marketplace card and detail page.

fingerprint.title: Image path creates a stronger fingerprint
fingerprint.body: Manifest hash, image digest, audit report and on-chain token bind a concrete version and reduce impersonation or silent replacement risk.

privacy.title: Private material should stay private
privacy.body: API keys, private prompts, internal hosts and source code should not go into the README or a public manifest. Use secrets management and private image credentials later.

help.label: Docker image guidance
help.title: Why AgentLens encourages image submission
help.body: Submitting an image is not submitting source code. AgentLens encourages private image submission because it makes audits reproducible, verifiable and bound to a specific version while preserving closed-source implementation.

benefits.fingerprint: Get a stronger Agent identity fingerprint: manifest hash + image digest + audit report + on-chain token.
benefits.audit: Let the sandbox verify network access, RPC boundaries, resource usage and abnormal behavior.
benefits.ranking: Passed audits are better suited for higher Trust Tiers, recommendations and high-trust rankings.
benefits.disputes: If a dispute happens, AgentLens can rerun the same version and check for privilege abuse or version swaps.

recommended.highRisk: The Agent touches funds, trading, enterprise permissions, sensitive data or autonomous actions.
recommended.trustTier: You want an audit badge, strong fingerprint and higher trust tier.
recommended.platformLease: You want the platform to provide a fuller rental, access, review and reputation loop.

notRequired.closedSource: Your closed-source SaaS is still validating demand and only exposes API/MCP for now.
notRequired.earlyListing: You only want a marketplace listing first to test demand and pricing.
notRequired.externalSaas: The Agent runs entirely in your own cloud and AgentLens only provides discovery and gateway access.

readme.title: Public README / marketplace listing
readme.description: This is what end users see for search, recommendations, detail pages and rental decisions. Do not include API keys, private prompts, internal hosts or source code details.
readme.summaryPlaceholder: This Agent turns on-chain risk events into actionable research summaries.
readme.useCasesPlaceholder:
DeFi risk monitoring
Market research
Investment summaries
readme.capabilitiesPlaceholder:
Read public data sources
Generate structured reports
Output risk signals
readme.limitationsPlaceholder:
Does not custody funds
Does not sign transactions for users
readme.examplePlaceholder:
Input: analyze protocol risk events over the last 24 hours
Output: event summary, affected scope, suggested actions
readme.ok: The public listing has the minimum required marketplace details.

validation.useCases: Add at least one use case.

manifest.title: Audit manifest / image material
manifest.description: These fields are for the platform sandbox. The image can be a private registry reference and should later use a short-lived pull credential. Never put secrets in the manifest.

hosted.title: Hosted API/MCP integration
hosted.description: Without an image, you run the Agent yourself. AgentLens can still provide gateway access control, metering, health checks, black-box tests and dynamic reputation events.
hosted.authMethodPlaceholder: Platform-held API key / OAuth client / no auth
hosted.submitDescription: This path saves a hosted Agent draft first. After confirming the material, submit it for black-box review. The local MVP now includes approval, secrets, test leases, gateway invocation and metering summaries.
hosted.savedBody: Draft ID: {{hostedAgentId}}. You can submit it for black-box review; rentals still require approval and gateway integration.

review.title: Pending review created
review.healthcheckMissing: Not configured; endpoint needs manual validation
review.draftMissing: Save the hosted Agent draft first.
review.noteFingerprint: AgentLens generated a hosted identity fingerprint from the README, endpoint, schema and developer address.
review.noteReview: This path has no image, so it starts with black-box review and should rank below audited image submissions.
review.noteGateway: After review, use the local MVP panel to approve, configure secrets, create a test lease and verify the Gateway.

gateway.title: Gateway local MVP
gateway.description: Validates the no-image rental loop: approval, platform-held secrets, lease tokens, proxy invocation, rate limits and metering. This is not the final payment, split or refund system.

secret.note: The local MVP stores downstream secrets in runtime JSON. Production should replace this with KMS/Vault/managed Secret Manager and admin authorization.

lease.tokenNote: The token is returned only at creation time; the backend stores only a hash. Real rentals should create leases from payment or credit orders.

messages.approved: Hosted Agent approved in the local MVP.
messages.leaseCreated: Test lease created. You can use the token through the Gateway.
messages.invoked: Gateway invocation completed and metering summary refreshed.

errors.draftMissing: Save the hosted Agent draft first.
errors.reviewMissing: Submit and complete the black-box review record first.
errors.secretMissing: Add the downstream auth header and secret.
errors.gatewayNotReady: Approve the Agent and configure the Gateway secret first.
errors.leaseMissing: Create a test lease token first.
errors.durationHours: Lease duration must be an integer between 1 and 720.
errors.maxRequests: Max requests must be an integer between 1 and 100000.
errors.maxRequestsPerMinute: Per-minute limit must be an integer between 1 and 1000.
errors.payloadJson: Invocation payload must be valid JSON.

validation.title: Hosted integration validation
validation.ok: Hosted API/MCP material is ready for a draft.
validation.fixHosted: Complete the hosted API/MCP integration material first.
validation.apiMissing: Hosted Agent API URL is not configured, so the draft cannot be saved.
validation.endpointUrl: Add a valid http(s) Agent endpoint.
validation.schemaUrl: Add a valid http(s) schema URL.
validation.healthcheckUrl: Healthcheck URL must be valid http(s), or left blank.

validation.title: Manifest validation
validation.ok: Manifest shape is valid for the current audit sandbox.
validation.stakeTooLow: Stake amount must cover serviceFee + minimumBond.
validation.stakeInvalid: Stake override must be a valid ETH amount.
validation.fixManifest: Fix manifest validation errors before staking.
validation.fixProfile: Complete the public README / marketplace listing first.
validation.image: image must be a non-empty Docker image reference.
validation.allowedHostsRequired: allowed_hosts must include at least one public host.
validation.allowedHostsPrivate: allowed_hosts cannot include private, loopback, or host-machine targets.
validation.allowedHostsPath: allowed_hosts should be hostnames, not paths or URLs.
validation.rpcRequired: allowed_rpc_endpoints must include at least one http(s) endpoint.
validation.rpcProtocol: allowed_rpc_endpoints must use http or https.
validation.rpcPrivate: allowed_rpc_endpoints cannot include private or loopback targets.
validation.manifestUrlInvalid: manifestUrl must be a valid public http(s) URL.
validation.manifestUrlProtocol: manifestUrl must use http or https so the audit listener can fetch it.

wallet.title: Wallet and stake
wallet.minimumNote: Default stake is now set to the smallest test unit. Before mainnet or real fund flow, switch to risk-based bonding.
wallet.pricingMissing: Registry pricing is not loaded yet.
wallet.walletError: Wallet connection is unavailable or was rejected.

preview.title: Submission preview
preview.description: Public README and technical submission material are separated so they can later feed the marketplace detail page and audit system independently.
